basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of data engineering experience
- 3+ years of data warehouse technical architectures, data modeling, infrastructure components, ETL/ ELT and reporting/analytic tools and environments, data structures and hands-on SQL coding experience
- Experience with AWS technologies like Redshift, S3, AWS Glue, EMR, Kinesis, FireHose, Lambda, and IAM roles and permissions
- Experience using business intelligence reporting tools such as OBIEE, Business Objects, Cognos, Tableau, MicroStrategy, SSAS Cubes, etc.
- Experience with any ETL tool like, Informatica, ODI, SSIS, Datastage, etc.
- Experience with programming languages like Python, Pyspark or equivalent
- Experience in reverse engineering legacy analytical/reporting applications where minimal documentation is available.
description:
Amazon's Worldwide Grocery Stores-Grocery Tech Foundation team is seeking an experienced Data Engineer to lead medium to large cross-functional strategic initiatives that support WWGS strategic goals. This is a unique opportunity for someone inspired by WWGS core values and is interested in Amazon’s high-growth grocery business to bring their whole self to the role.

Key job responsibilities
- Participate in on-call rotation to provide support for custom-built reporting and analytical applications, ensuring business-as-usual operations and timely data delivery for business users.
- Lead data integration, reporting, and analytics projects, leveraging experience in enterprise data engineering.
- Understand business requirements, design and implement scalable data model, ETL processes and reporting to address business needs.
- Design and build next-generation data infrastructure to replace existing third-party solutions.
- Collaborate cross-functionally with Data Engineers, Software Developers, Business Intelligence Engineers, Analysts, Product Managers, and Program Managers.
- Transform complex datasets into automated, digestible analytic deliverables.
- Demonstrate strong organizational skills, decisiveness, and ownership while working in a team environment.
- Drive significant impact through modernized design, architecture, and development of data products.
- Contribute technical expertise to help transition the organization from legacy solutions to future-state data infrastructure.
- Partner with data engineering team members to deliver on strategic vision and objectives.

A day in the life
Ensure business continuity and on-time data delivery to business users. Work with your leadership to define and execute the reporting and analytics strategy for WWGST. This will involve leveraging your Data Modeling, SQL and coding skills in languages like Python, Pyspark and Java to extract, transform, and load data. You will collaborate closely with both internal and external teams to scope, design, build, and support efficient and cost-effective data pipelines focused on performance, operational excellence, and delivering business value. This work will span multiple technology stacks, including a custom-built proprietary Amazon framework. You will participate in and lead standups, design sessions, and Jira management, creating necessary documentation and artifacts. Additionally, you will have the opportunity to lead and mentor your peers.

About the team
The Data Platform Management (DPM) team under Grocery Tech Foundation (GTF) is responsible for data and reporting solutions for Grocery business users. We enable sophisticated, fact-based decision making across WWGST by delivering constantly evolving technology and analytic capabilities. This team will solve challenging and novel problems every day while collaborating with multiple teams across WWGST. You will be able to enjoy and contribute to the unique blend of Amazon and WFM Tech culture.
description_short: Amazon's Worldwide Grocery Stores-Grocery Tech Foundation team is seeking an experienced Data Engineer to lead medium to large cross-functional strategic initiatives that support WWGS strategic goals.

description:
Amazon Freight is a startup business within Amazon focused on developing an industry-leading transportation offering to provide external freight Customers access to the world-class network and freight services currently used to facilitate Amazon’s own freight movements. Our freight offerings/portfolio ensures our Customer’s freight is moved with the highest level of service and quality at a competitive price across multiple modes of transportation, including truckload, less-than-truckload, and intermodal. In doing this, Amazon is deeply studying customer shipping needs and building innovative, market-leading solutions that will organically attract customers towards our services.

As a Sr. Data Engineer in Amazon Freight Data Analytics team, you will
· Build robust and scalable data integration (ETL) pipelines using SQL, EMR, Python and Spark.
· Build and deliver high quality data architecture to support business intelligence engineer and program managers’ reporting needs.
· Interface with other technology teams to extract, transform, and load data from a wide variety of data sources.
· Drive both business and technology solutions to improve Amazon Inbound visibility.
· Continually improve ongoing reporting and analysis processes, automating or simplifying self-service support for customers.
· Translate data into actionable insights for the stakeholders.

description:
The North American Stores Expansion Team is seeking an experienced and motivated Data Engineer to work on driving strategic projects across multiple geographies. We collaborate with Retail, SCOT (Supply Chain Optimization Technologies), AIT (Amazon Inbound Transportation), Vendor Negotiations, and Canada Product teams to develop scalable analytics applications, AI/ML products, and research models that optimize operations and enable business expansion. The environment is fast-paced with constant new challenges to solve.

The ideal candidate will have good leadership skills, proven ability to develop, enhance, automate, and manage analytics models and data pipelines. The candidate will have strong data mining and modeling skills and will be comfortable facilitating idea creation and working from concept through to execution. This role will also build tools and support structures needed to analyze data and dive deep to determine root causes of systems errors. The candidate will also have the opportunity to present findings to cross functional team partners to drive improvements. The ideal candidate will have a proven ability to work with Applied Scientists, Data Scientists, Software Development Engineers, Product Managers and Business Partners to solve challenging analytical problems. The candidate must be comfortable using intellect, curiosity and technical ability to develop innovative solutions to business problems. The position requires ability to learn different aspects of the business and understand how to apply advanced analytics to solve high impact problems.

A qualified candidate must have demonstrated ability to manage medium and large scale automation and modeling projects, identify requirements and build methodology and tools that are mathematically grounded but also explainable operationally, apply technical skills allowing the models to adapt to changing attributes. Strong written and verbal communication skills are a must as this role will be in frequent contact with leadership. The person in this role will be expected to provide clear and concise explanation to results and approaches as well as provide opinion and guidance on problem solving.

Key job responsibilities
Design, develop and maintain scalable data models, ETL/ELT processes, and ML pipelines using AWS services (S3, Redshift, Glue, Athena, Lake Formation)

Analyze business data to optimize key processes in Retail and Supply Chain domains

Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Scientists, Engineers, Program Managers) to deliver data solutions and implement predictive models

Implement and maintain CI/CD pipelines while ensuring data governance, security, and compliance standards

Monitor, optimize, and enhance data infrastructure to support analytics and machine learning

Stay current with emerging technologies and mentor junior engineers in data engineering and MLOps best practices

description:
The Central Learning Solutions - Technology team builds state-of-the-art Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions for enhancing leadership and associate development within the organization. We develop products and mechanisms for measuring the impact of learning programs, improving the training content and recommending the personalized learning courses based on the post-training performance curves for different learner profiles.



Key job responsibilities
As a Data Enablement team within Central Learning Solutions, our key responsibilities encompass creating a single source of truth for both structured and unstructured data. This includes building comprehensive data lakes and data warehouses that enable science and AI applications to consume reliable data.

Our core focus areas include:

Data Infrastructure: Maintaining robust pipelines and frameworks that ensure validated, true-source data with end-to-end documentation and tracking
Self-Service Analytics: Producing proactive insights and vending actionable outcomes to customers
Data Quality: Implementing validation mechanisms at each infrastructure layer to ensure trustable business insights
The Data Engineering team consists of four engineers working collaboratively to deliver highly reliable data infrastructure. We enable data access from multiple sources with real-time to near real-time ingestion, integrating learning data across systems to create a central repository for all LMS data, Kirkpatrick metrics, and L4 quality metrics.

This role involves working alongside three other engineers to build and maintain the data infrastructure that powers analytical consumption for Data Science teams, BI teams, SDE teams, and external line of business partners.

A day in the life
You'll independently work on user stories while coordinating with Software Development Engineering and Data Science teams on workflows, design, and data layer patterns. Collaborating with TPMs and Product Managers, you'll ensure data requirements are met while working within the single source of truth architecture.

Your responsibilities include maintaining data lakes, producing automation and orchestration tools, and contributing to build and launch exercises. Operating in sprints, you'll independently lead your assigned area, work on roadmaps, and cross-collaborate outside CLS Analytics teams to drive data engineering products and maintain infrastructure.

About the team
Central Learning Solutions Tech is a dedicated analytics, data engineering, data science, and software development team working with worldwide operations to build products that enable content creators to operate efficiently. We provide AI-driven insights and value to learners (under-the-roof associates ) , driving productivity and improving learning outcomes. Our team collaborates closely with content creators to deliver direct value for associates across the world through the products we create.

description:
We are looking for a Data Platform Engineer to build the foundational data systems that power our robotics and machine learning development. In this role, you will design and implement the infrastructure for collecting, storing, processing, and transforming the vast amounts of data generated by our robots—from sensor telemetry and video streams to operational logs and performance metrics.
You'll work closely with ML teams to ensure data is accessible, well-structured, and ready for training. Your work will enable research teams to iterate faster and operations teams to monitor fleet performance as we scale.

Key job responsibilities
- Design and build scalable data pipelines for ingesting and processing robotics data (sensor streams, video, telemetry, logs)
- Develop and maintain data storage solutions optimized for diverse data types and access patterns
- Create tools and APIs for researchers and engineers to efficiently query and analyze large datasets
- Build real-time data processing systems for monitoring robot fleet performance
- Build and maintain data transformation pipelines that prepare robotics data for ML training
- Collaborate with ML and robotics teams to ensure data platforms meet their evolving needs

About the team
Fauna Robotics, an Amazon company, is building capable, safe, and genuinely delightful robots for everyday life. Our goal is simple: make robots people actually want to live and interact with in everyday human spaces.
We believe that future won’t arrive until building for robotics becomes far more accessible. Today, too much effort is spent reinventing the fundamentals. We’re changing that by developing tightly integrated hardware and software systems that make it faster, safer, and more intuitive to create real-world robotic products.
Our work spans the full stack: mechanical design, control systems, dynamic modeling, and intelligent software. The focus is not just functionality, but experience. We’re building robots that feel responsive, expressive, and genuinely useful.
At Fauna, you’ll work at the frontier of this space, helping define how robots move, manipulate, and interact with people in natural environments. It’s an opportunity to solve hard problems across hardware and software with a team focused on making robotics accessible and joyful to build.
If you care about making robotics real for everyone and building systems that are as delightful as they are capable, we’re interested in hearing from you.

basic_qualifications:

- Experience with SQL
description:
Come build the future of entertainment with us. Are you interested in shaping the future of movies and television? Do you want to define the next generation of how and what Amazon customers are watching?

Prime Video is a premium streaming service that offers customers a vast collection of TV shows and movies - all with the ease of finding what they love to watch in one place. We offer customers thousands of popular movies and TV shows including Amazon Originals and exclusive licensed content to exciting live sports events. We also offer our members the opportunity to subscribe to add-on channels which they can cancel at anytime and to rent or buy new release movies and TV box sets on the Prime Video Store. Prime Video is a fast-paced, growth business - available in over 200 countries and territories worldwide. The team works in a dynamic environment where innovating on behalf of our customers is at the heart of everything we do. If this sounds exciting to you, please read on.


Key job responsibilities
This is an impactful role within Prime Video (PV) Commerce Analytics, supporting major PV investments in 2026. As a Data Engineer, you will work in one of the world's largest and most complex data warehouse environments, designing and implementing scalable data infrastructure solutions that integrate multiple heterogeneous data sources.

You will leverage AWS big data technologies including Redshift, EMR/Spark, Andes Data Lake, and native AWS services to build and maintain data pipelines that ingest data from Video Commerce, Digital Commerce, Prime, and Device Platform teams. Your curated datasets will serve 50+ downstream teams for reporting, analysis, machine learning models, and ad-hoc requests.

You will build high-performing, scalable data products that directly enable data-driven decision making across Prime Video.

Key responsibilities include:
• Design complex data pipelines and models powering subscription, transaction, and device registration datasets
• Deliver scalable data solutions on native AWS infrastructure
• Develop data quality validation frameworks and monitoring solutions ensuring data integrity and timeliness
• Build and optimize batch and near real-time processing workflows using Spark, Redshift, and Andes
• Create robust orchestration and monitoring for workflows with complex dependencies
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to onboard new data sources


About the team
The team is primarily based in Seattle with additional presence in New York and London. A data-driven organization focused on Prime Video's growth and commerce analytics, with strong representation in business intelligence, data engineering, and product management functions supporting Prime Video's commercial and analytical needs.

Cross-functional team building intelligent systems that integrate content and data to deliver personalized customer experiences across streaming, live events, and emerging formats.

description:
Employer: Amazon.com Services LLC
Position: Data Engineer III - AMZ27926.1
Location: Austin, TX

Multiple Positions Available:

Design, develop, implement, test, document, and operate large-scale, high-volume, high-performance data structures for business intelligence analytics. Implement data structures using best practices in data modeling, ETL/ELT processes, SQL, Oracle, and OLAP technologies. Provide on-line reporting and analysis using OBIEE business intelligence tools and a logical abstraction layer against large, multi-dimensional datasets and multiple sources. Gather business and functional requirements and translate these requirements into robust, scalable, operable solutions that work well within the overall data architecture. Analyze source data systems and drive best practices in source teams. Participate in the full development life cycle, end-to-end, from design, implementation and testing, to documentation, delivery, support, and maintenance. Produce comprehensive, usable dataset documentation and metadata. Evaluate and make decisions around dataset implementations designed and proposed by peer data engineers. Evaluate and make decisions around the use of new or existing software products and tools. Mentor junior data engineers.

(40 hours / week, 8:00am-5:00pm, Salary Range $174533 - $209100)

description:
Title: Data Engineer

Organization Overview:
In Kindle, authors are at the center of everything we do. It's that simple. We have a great team that thrives on innovation aand our staff is talented, driven, and passionate about building an exceptional customer experience. If you want to make a real difference for customers and tackle complex business problems with data, the Data Engineer role is the job for you!

DESCRIPTION
The Risk Operations, Success, & Engagement (ROSE) Business Intelligence team is looking for an experienced, self-driven, and analytical Data Engineer with expertise in AWS services and a strong aptitude for solving unstructured problems. In this role, you will work in a highly scalable, self-service designed infrastructure, owning end-to-end data engineering solutions. You should be passionate about working with large datasets, comfortable with ambiguity, and able to model multidimensional data and build the right data pipelines, process automations, and data solutions to turn hard questions into sustainable, self-serviceable answers

The right candidate will possess strong business and communication skills and the ability to partner effectively with business partners and BIEs to collaborate on building the right data solutions. They will be proficient with data engineering tools to build efficient, flexible, and scalable data models, pipelines, and infrastructure, with a desire to learn and implement new technologies and services to further the scalable, self-service model.

Key job responsibilities
As a Data Engineer on the ROSE BI team, you will bring together data engineering, cloud infrastructure, and business impact. You will take ownership of complex, end to end data solutions built on AWS, working closely with business partners, BIEs and data engineers to design, build, and scale the data foundation that powers our team's products and services. This includes owning projects that build new data pipelines, migrate existing ones, and refine current infrastructure to deliver more value for our customers.

In this role, your responsibilities will include:
- Manage AWS resources including S3, EC2, Redshift, Kinesis, SQS, EMR, Lambda, Glue, Step Functions, Bedrock and others.

- Drive architectural and design choices with innovation at the core, continually improving data solutions and enabling process automations and self-service capabilities that deliver impact to the business.

- Build and deliver high quality data architecture and pipelines to support BIEs and customer data needs.

- Interface with other technology teams to extract, transform, and load data from a wide variety of data sources.

- Actively participate in code reviews, design discussions, team planning, and operational excellence.

title: Data Engineer, Risk Operations, Success, & Engagement Business Intelligence
team.label: team-kindlecontent

basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of data engineering experience
- 3+ years of data warehouse technical architectures, data modeling, infrastructure components, ETL/ ELT and reporting/analytic tools and environments, data structures and hands-on SQL coding experience
- Experience with AWS technologies like Redshift, S3, AWS Glue, EMR, Kinesis, FireHose, Lambda, and IAM roles and permissions
- Experience using business intelligence reporting tools such as OBIEE, Business Objects, Cognos, Tableau, MicroStrategy, SSAS Cubes, etc.
- Experience with any ETL tool like, Informatica, ODI, SSIS, Datastage, etc.
- Experience with programming languages like Python, Pyspark or equivalent
- Experience in reverse engineering legacy analytical/reporting applications where minimal documentation is available.
description:
Amazon's Worldwide Grocery Stores-Grocery Tech Foundation team is seeking an experienced Data Engineer to lead medium to large cross-functional strategic initiatives that support WWGS strategic goals. This is a unique opportunity for someone inspired by WWGS core values and is interested in Amazon’s high-growth grocery business to bring their whole self to the role.

Key job responsibilities
- Participate in on-call rotation to provide support for custom-built reporting and analytical applications, ensuring business-as-usual operations and timely data delivery for business users.
- Lead data integration, reporting, and analytics projects, leveraging experience in enterprise data engineering.
- Understand business requirements, design and implement scalable data model, ETL processes and reporting to address business needs.
- Design and build next-generation data infrastructure to replace existing third-party solutions.
- Collaborate cross-functionally with Data Engineers, Software Developers, Business Intelligence Engineers, Analysts, Product Managers, and Program Managers.
- Transform complex datasets into automated, digestible analytic deliverables.
- Demonstrate strong organizational skills, decisiveness, and ownership while working in a team environment.
- Drive significant impact through modernized design, architecture, and development of data products.
- Contribute technical expertise to help transition the organization from legacy solutions to future-state data infrastructure.
- Partner with data engineering team members to deliver on strategic vision and objectives.

A day in the life
Ensure business continuity and on-time data delivery to business users. Work with your leadership to define and execute the reporting and analytics strategy for WWGST. This will involve leveraging your Data Modeling, SQL and coding skills in languages like Python, Pyspark and Java to extract, transform, and load data. You will collaborate closely with both internal and external teams to scope, design, build, and support efficient and cost-effective data pipelines focused on performance, operational excellence, and delivering business value. This work will span multiple technology stacks, including a custom-built proprietary Amazon framework. You will participate in and lead standups, design sessions, and Jira management, creating necessary documentation and artifacts. Additionally, you will have the opportunity to lead and mentor your peers.

About the team
The Data Platform Management (DPM) team under Grocery Tech Foundation (GTF) is responsible for data and reporting solutions for Grocery business users. We enable sophisticated, fact-based decision making across WWGST by delivering constantly evolving technology and analytic capabilities. This team will solve challenging and novel problems every day while collaborating with multiple teams across WWGST. You will be able to enjoy and contribute to the unique blend of Amazon and WFM Tech culture.
